title: Economics Fundamentals 101
description: A comprehensive introductory test covering basic economic principles, history, supply and demand, inflation, and policy tools.

questions.text: What is the fundamental problem that economics seeks to solve?
What is 'Opportunity Cost'?
options: The price of a movie ticket
The value of the next best alternative foregone
A discount given by a store

questions.text: Which branch of economics studies the behavior of individual households and firms?

What is a 'Market' in economics?
options: Any place where buyers and sellers exchange goods

questions.text: Which economic system relies on customs and history to decide what to produce?

What is 'Macroeconomics'?
options: The study of the economy as a whole

options: It can be replaced naturally over time

questions.text: What does 'Utility' mean in economics?
options: The satisfaction or usefulness received from a good

What was the 'Laissez-faire' approach?
options: No government interference in the economy

options: Nations should trade with each other

options: The cost of the first item
The cost of producing one additional unit

options: A general increase in prices over time
An increase in the value of money

options: Extremely rapid and out-of-control price increases

options: An increase in production costs (like oil prices)

options: The economy is in a depression

options: The total value of all goods and services produced in a country
The total amount of gold in a bank
The total debt of a nation

questions.text: Mercantilism was an economic theory that focused on:
options: Accumulating gold and maintaining a trade surplus

options: Increasing government spending or cutting taxes

questions.text: A 'Budget Deficit' occurs when:
options: Spending is more than tax revenue

questions.text: The 'National Debt' is the:
options: Money owed by citizens to banks
Total accumulation of all past budget deficits

questions.text: To slow down an overheating economy and high inflation, the government might:

options: Rich people pay a higher percentage
Everyone pays the same percentage of income
Poor people pay a higher percentage

questions.text: A 'Progressive Tax' system means:
options: Higher income earners pay a higher percentage
Lower income earners pay a higher percentage

questions.text: What is a 'Subsidy'?
options: A government payment to support a business or industry

questions.text: The 'Multiplier Effect' suggests that:
options: Government spending leads to a larger increase in total economic output

options: Buying and selling stocks in companies
Buying and selling government bonds to control money supply
The opening hours of the stock exchange

questions.text: The 'Reserve Requirement' is:
options: The amount of money the government must save
The percentage of deposits banks must keep on hand
The amount of gold a person can own

questions.text: If the Fed 'lowers' the reserve requirement, the money supply will:

What is the 'Discount Rate'?
options: A sale at a department store
The interest rate the Fed charges banks for loans
